steps.title: Identify the appositive
steps.content: The sentence refers to fellow OECD nations Costa Rica and Switzerland. Costa Rica identifies one of the nations in the phrase, and that restrictive naming does not need punctuation.

steps.title: Keep punctuation out
steps.content: A uses a colon as if introducing an explanation or list, C makes the country names nonrestrictive with a comma, and D uses a semicolon before a phrase that is not an independent clause. No punctuation is needed before Costa Rica. The answer is B.
